articles.title: Gotta Catch ’em All! The Best Pokémon Collabs to Shop for the 30th Anniversary
articles.content:
几周前，我发现自己在一个周六早上7点30分就排在了Target超市门外。不，我不是在提前处理周末的杂务。我和几十位其他粉丝一样，是为了抢购宝可梦与Target的联名产品，庆祝该IP的30周年纪念。（我承认，我就是一心想要一顶皮卡丘训练师帽！）

2026年标志着原版游戏问世三十周年——各大品牌正通过联名商品、主题美食和家居用品帮助粉丝们庆祝。以下是目前可以购买的一些顶级宝可梦联名产品。

不过要提醒一句：这些产品往往售罄很快。如果你想全部入手，就得尽快下手，并留意补货信息。

这个Target产品系列相当全面，涵盖服装、美妆和家居用品，面向各类粉丝。第一波于5月2日发售，目前基本售罄。但第二波将于6月6日在门店上架。我超喜欢那个看起来像精灵球的躲避球——完美契合90年代复古夏日风！

准备好接受挑战了吗？这些乐高积木套装细节精致，非常适合有耐心的粉丝。套装共有三款可选：伊布、妙蛙草、喷火龙和水箭龟，或者皮卡丘和精灵球。预计今年晚些时候还会推出更多套装。

如果你在找宝可梦T恤，优衣库的联名系列有儿童和成人款可供选择。该系列将于6月15日发售，T恤采用经典电子游戏画面，呈现喷火龙、皮卡丘、耿鬼和卡比兽等最具标志性的角色。

想要更多可穿戴的周边？试试彪马与宝可梦的联名系列。没错，既有儿童款也有成人款运动鞋。此外还有T恤、卫衣、运动服、短裤等多种选择。这个系列已经上市一段时间，部分尺码已经断货。

训练这么多宝可梦可是大工程！如果你的确也练饿了，金鱼饼干品牌正好推出了四款庆祝初代御三家的新饼干：小火龙、杰尼龟、妙蛙草和皮卡丘。

如果你计划今年夏天去海滩，可以一边保持凉爽一边展示宝可梦风格。这个Igloo冷藏箱系列并非新品，但确实有一些我见过的最可爱的设计，包括精灵球斜挎小冰箱和角色易拉罐保温套。

如果你真的想要全部收藏，好时巧克力推出了主题包装的甜蜜巧克力。每一颗巧克力看起来都像精灵球，翻到底部则会露出初代151只宝可梦之一。
articles.summary:
A few weeks ago, I found myself lining up outside of Target at 7:30 a.m. on a Saturday. No, I wasn’t there to get a jumpstart on weekend errands. I, along with dozens of other fans, was there to catch the Pokémon x Target drop in celebration of the franchise’s 30th anniversary. (What can I say, I had my heart set on a Psyduck Trainer Cap!)

2026 marks three decades since the original game debuted—and brands are helping fans celebrate with merch, themed foods and home goods. Below are some of the top Pokémon collabs you can shop now.

One word of warning, though? These drops tend to sell out fast. You’ll want to shop quickly and keep an eye out for restocks if you plan to catch ’em all.

Psst! See what financial experts have to say about whether Pokémon cards are a good investment.

Top Pokémon collabs in 2026

Pokémon x Target

Shop on Target

This well-rounded Target collection includes something for every fan, including clothing, beauty and home goods. The first drop, which landed on May 2, is pretty much sold out. But the second wave is landing in stores on June 6. I’m obsessed with the kickball that looks like a Poké Ball—perfect for a retro ’90s summer!

Pokémon x LEGO

Shop on LEGO

Up for a challenge? These highly detailed LEGO sets are perfect for fans with a little patience. Choose from three sets: Eevee, Venusaur, Charizard and Blastoise or Pikachu and Poké Ball. Additional sets are expected to land later this year.

Pokémon x Uniqlo

Shop on Uniqlo

If you’re searching for a Pokémon T-shirt, the Uniqulo collab has options for kids and adults alike. Dropping on June 15, this line of tees includes original video game art of some of the most iconic characters, including Charizard, Pikachu, Gengar and Snorlax.

Pokémon x PUMA

Shop on PUMA

For even more wearable merch, try the PUMA x Pokémon collab. Yes, there are sneakers in both kid and adult sizes. Plus, T-shirts, hoodies, jerseys, bottoms and more. This collection has been out for a while, so some sizes are limited.

Pokémon x Goldfish Crackers

Shop Pikachu Crackers

Shop Bulbasaur Crackers

Shop Squirtle Crackers

Shop Charmander Crackers

Training all these Pokémon is a tall task! If you, too, have worked up an appetite, Goldfish has you covered. The kids’ snack brand just dropped four new crackers celebrating the original starters: Charmander, Squirtle, Bulbasaur and Pikachu.

Pokémon x Igloo

Shop on Igloo

If you’re planning some beach days this summer, stay cool while showing off some Pokémon flair. This Igloo Coolers collection isn’t new, but it does feature some of the cutest designs I’ve seen, including a Poké Ball crossbody cooler and character can holders.

Pokémon x Hershey's Kisses

Shop on Walmart

If you truly want to catch ’em all, Hershey’s dropped their sweet chocolate kisses in themed wrappers. Each kiss looks like a Poké Ball, while the bottom unveils one of the original 151 Pokémon.

The post Gotta Catch ’em All! The Best Pokémon Collabs to Shop for the 30th Anniversary appeared first on Reader's Digest.

articles.title: This Is the No. 1 Mistake You’re Making with Your Allergy Meds—And It’s Actually Making Your Sniffles Worse!
articles.content:
每年春天都以相同的方式开始：一个轻微的吸鼻子声，紧接着是一阵猛烈的喷嚏攻势，感觉你的身体想把吸入的花粉重新喷回大气中。然后是眼睛发痒、无法用一个鼻孔然后两个鼻孔吸气，以及口袋里和包里塞满的纸巾。听起来很熟悉？你并不孤单。

超过八千万美国人患有季节性过敏，也称为过敏性鼻炎或花粉热。“这个时节，最大的罪魁祸首通常是树木和草类的花粉，”新罕布什尔州黎巴嫩的达特茅斯希区柯克医学中心的过敏症专科医生和临床免疫学家许安荣医学博士说。它们会在眼睛和鼻子引发炎症，并激活免疫系统中的过敏抗体。“当这些抗体再次接触花粉时，”他补充道，“它们会导致过敏细胞破裂，释放出能引起打喷嚏、流鼻涕和眼睛流泪的化学物质。”

讽刺的是，这些恼人的症状实际上是你身体试图通过排出过敏原来保护你的一部分。你已经知道这个建议：不要等到症状出现；在过敏季开始之前就开始服用过敏药物，抢在花粉前面。也许你今年甚至听从了建议，几周前就开始服药了——然而不知为何，你仍然很难受，纳闷在积极用药的情况下怎么还能这么鼻塞。

我们采访了许博士和另外两位过敏症专科医生，他们都认同：一个出人意料的常见错误可能正在阻止你获得真正的缓解。在某些情况下，这个错误甚至可能使症状恶化。继续阅读，了解你可能做错了什么，以及让你很快就能轻松呼吸的简单解决方法。

“用鼻喷剂的'吸鼻'动作，”印第安纳州华沙市帕克维尤健康中心的委员会认证过敏症专科医生和免疫学家朱莉娅·L·蒙特霍医学博士说。现在，问题不在喷剂本身（稍后会介绍哪种最好）——而是你使用它们的方式。许博士解释说，本能的反应是把鼻喷剂直接对准鼻子喷，“但那样药物就不会到达需要去的地方。”

西弗吉尼亚州巴伯斯维尔的委员会认证过敏症专科医生和免疫学家梅根·W·谢泼德医学博士补充说，如果把喷嘴对准鼻中隔或鼻子中部也会有问题。那样会把药物送进喉咙，而不是药物本应治疗的炎症组织。蒙特霍博士说：“如果你立刻尝到了味道，那就是浪费了。”

这个错误非常常见，因为大多数人的医疗提供者从未教过他们如何正确使用鼻喷剂，而且他们也不清楚药物实际上应该到达哪里。许博士解释道：“如果药物使用不正确，许多人会说药物不起作用，然后就停止使用。然而，这些鼻喷剂，特别是类固醇和抗组胺鼻喷剂，是治疗季节性过敏的一线药物，甚至可能比口服过敏药片更有效。”

除了不能给你所需的缓解外，错误使用喷剂实际上会让你感觉更糟。谢泼德博士指出：“如果药物反复冲击鼻中隔，更容易刺激脆弱的血管，增加流鼻血的风险，同时也会使药物对鼻塞和流涕的效果降低。”

我们采访的所有过敏症专科医生都推荐类似的技术来帮助药物到达正确的位置。他们称之为“鼻向脚尖”。

第一步：使用鼻喷剂时，轻轻低头向前倾斜，这样你就能看到自己的脚尖。

第二步：每侧鼻孔使用对侧的手——例如，左手拿着瓶子喷右鼻孔，反之亦然——将喷嘴朝向远离鼻子中线的方向，指向眼角的内部。

第三步：在此过程中正常吸气——不要用力吸或喷。过度用力会把药物送进喉咙而不是让它停留在需要治疗的炎症鼻组织上。

错误使用鼻喷剂并非过敏症专科医生在过敏季节看到的唯一错误。以下是其他一些需要避免的常见错误。

许博士警告不要过度依赖含羟甲唑啉的鼻喷剂，这类药物通常被称为阿弗林。“这是一种能迅速减轻鼻部肿胀的减充血剂，但无法治疗过敏的根本原因，”他说。“此外，一旦停用会导致反弹性鼻塞，长期使用（超过三天）会产生'成瘾'效应。”

一个出人意料的有用工具？盐水冲洗。许博士说，使用喷雾或冲洗设备如洗鼻壶或冲洗瓶进行鼻部盐水冲洗，是清除花粉、黏液和其他刺激物的有效无药物疗法。

蒙特霍博士说：“这是一种价格低廉的非处方抗组胺药，在一定程度上可能有效。但苯海拉明只能维持四到六小时，其常见副作用包括镇静/疲劳和口干，这限制了它的实用性。”她解释说，单纯患季节性过敏就可能因为多种原因导致明显的疲劳。“你的免疫系统超负荷运转，释放组胺和细胞因子，引发炎症、扰乱睡眠并消耗能量。”

再加上服用口服抗组胺药，这可能会让你在白天昏昏欲睡。蒙特霍博士说：“夜间鼻塞和鼻后滴漏会导致睡眠质量差，进而造成白天疲劳。”许博士更倾向于选择新型不引起嗜睡的选项，如西替利嗪（仙特明）、非索非那定（艾来）和左西替利嗪（赞家）。

谢泼德博士指出，许多人还误用了吸入器。这些定量吸入器是常见的手持“喷雾器”，用于治疗哮喘或过敏相关的呼吸症状。

吸入器应该始终配合储雾罐使用，这是一种轻质塑料管，一端连接吸入器，另一端有咬嘴。药物不会直接射入喉咙后部，而是暂时保存在储雾罐的腔室内，这样你可以更缓慢、更深地吸入肺部，到达真正需要的部位。

但许多人跳过储雾罐，直接把药物喷入口中。这意味着较少的药物到达气道——而更多的药物停留在口腔和喉咙，可能增加刺激和副作用。使用时，将吸入器连接到储雾罐，按一次吸入器将药物释放到腔室中，然后紧紧含住咬嘴，慢慢深吸。

对于长期缓解，我们的专家指向过敏原免疫疗法，通常称为脱敏针。脱敏针不是简单地掩盖症状，而是逐步训练免疫系统逐渐耐受过敏原。谢泼德博士说：“正确进行时，你基本上是在教你的身体以安全、非炎症的方式识别过敏原。”

虽然这周帮不上忙，但过敏症专科医生一致认为，过敏药物在症状加重之前开始使用时效果最佳。理想情况下，治疗应在过敏季节开始前两到四周开始。

如果非处方药物没有帮助、你的症状干扰了日常生活，或者你想要更针对性的治疗，医生建议去看委员会认证的过敏症专科医生进行测试和个性化治疗方案。
articles.summary:
Every spring starts the same way: one tiny sniffle, followed by a sneeze attack so aggressive, it feels like your body is trying to launch the pollen you’ve inhaled back into the atmosphere. Then come the itchy eyes, the inability to suck air through one and then both nostrils, and the tissues stuffed into every pocket and bag you own. Sound familiar? You’re not alone.

More than 80 million Americans suffer from seasonal allergies, also known as allergic rhinitis or hay fever. “This time of year, the biggest culprits are usually tree and grass pollens,” says An Huynh, MD, an allergist and clinical immunologist at Dartmouth Hitchcock Medical Center in Lebanon, New Hampshire. They trigger inflammation in the eyes and nose and activate allergic antibodies in the immune system. “The next time these antibodies come in contact with pollen,” he adds, “they can cause allergic cells to break open and release chemicals that can cause sneezing, sniffles and watery eyes.”

Ironically, those annoying symptoms are actually part of your body’s attempt to protect you by flushing allergens back out. You already know the advice: Don’t wait until symptoms hit; get ahead of the pollen and start taking your allergy medication before allergy season begins. Maybe you even listened this year and started your meds weeks ago—yet somehow, you’re still miserable, wondering how you can possibly be this congested while actively medicated.

We spoke to Dr. Huynh and two other allergists, and they all agreed: One surprisingly common mistake may be preventing you from getting real relief. In some cases, it can even make symptoms worse. Keep reading to discover what you may be doing wrong and the easy fix that will have you breathing easy in no time.

Get Reader’s Digest’s Read Up newsletter for more health advice, cleaning, humor, travel, tech and fun facts all week long.

What is the No. 1 mistake people make with their allergy meds?

“‘Snorting’ nasal sprays,” says Julia L. Montejo, MD, a board-certified allergist and immunologist at Parkview Health in Warsaw, Indiana. Now, the problem is not the sprays themselves (more on which ones are best coming up)—it’s the way you’re using them. The natural instinct is to point the nose spray directly up the nose, explains Dr. Huynh, “but then the medicine is not going where it needs to go.”

It’s also a problem if you aim the nozzle toward your septum, or the middle of the nose, adds Meagan W. Shepherd, MD, a board-certified allergist and immunologist in Barboursville, West Virginia. That can send the medicine into your throat instead of toward the inflamed tissue the medication is meant to treat. Dr. Montejo says, “If you immediately tasted it, you wasted it.”

The mistake is incredibly common because most people are never taught how to use nose sprays correctly by their health-care providers and are unfamiliar with where the medication is actually supposed to go, says Dr. Shepherd.

Why is this a problem?

“If the medication isn’t used correctly, many people will then say the medicine doesn’t work, and then they stop using it,” Dr. Huynh explains. “However, these nose sprays, specifically steroid and antihistamine nasal sprays, are first-line medications for managing seasonal allergies and can be even more effective than an oral allergy pill.”

Besides not giving you the relief you need, using the spray incorrectly can actually make you feel even worse. “If medication repeatedly hits the septum, it’s more likely to irritate delicate blood vessels, increasing the risk of nosebleeds while also making the medicine less effective against congestion and drainage,” notes Dr. Shepherd.

What’s the right way to use a nasal spray?

All the allergists we spoke to recommend a similar technique to help medication reach the right spot. They call it “nose to toes.”

Step 1: When using nasal sprays, bend your head forward slightly so you’re looking at your toes.

Step 2: Using the opposite hand for each nostril—for example, holding the bottle in your left hand to spray your right nostril, and vice versa—angle the nose spray nozzle away from the middle of your nose toward the inside corner of your eye.

Step 3: Inhale normally while doing this—don’t sniff or snort. Sniffing too aggressively can send the medication down your throat instead of allowing it to settle on the inflamed nasal tissue, where it’s needed most.

Other allergy-medication mistakes that can backfire

Using nasal sprays incorrectly isn’t the only mistake allergists see during allergy season. Here are some other common mistakes to avoid.

Using one particular nasal spray

Dr. Huynh warns against relying too heavily on oxymetazoline nasal sprays, commonly known as Afrin. “This is a decongestant that can decrease nasal swelling very quickly, but it does not treat the underlying cause of the allergies,” he says. “Plus, it can cause rebound congestion when stopped, leading to an ‘addictive’ effect with prolonged use (more than three days).”

A surprisingly helpful tool? Saline rinses. Using nasal saline in a spray or an irrigation method such as a neti pot or rinse bottle are terrific non-medicated therapies to help wash out pollen, mucus and other irritants, Dr. Huynh says.

Taking Benadryl

“It’s an inexpensive over-the-counter antihistamine that may be effective to a degree,” says Dr. Montejo. “But Benadryl only lasts four to six hours, and its common side effects include sedation/fatigue and dry mouth, which limit its usefulness.” Having seasonal allergies alone can cause a significant amount of fatigue for several reasons, she explains. “Your immune system works overtime, releasing histamines and cytokines that trigger inflammation, disrupt sleep and drain your energy.”

Add to that taking an oral antihistamine, which may make you drowsy during the day. “Nasal congestion and postnasal drainage at night cause poor-quality sleep, resulting in daytime fatigue,” Dr. Montejo says. Dr. Huynh prefers newer nondrowsy options such as cetirizine (Zyrtec), fexofenadine (Allegra) and levocetirizine (Xyzal).

Incorrectly using inhalers

According to Dr. Shepherd, many people also misuse their inhalers. These MDIs (metered-dose inhalers) are the classic handheld “puffer” inhaler that sprays a quick burst of medication for asthma or allergy-related breathing symptoms.

They should always be used with a spacer device, a lightweight plastic tube that attaches to the inhaler on one end and has a mouthpiece on the other. Instead of the medication shooting straight into the back of your throat, the spacer briefly holds the medicine in the chamber so you can inhale it more slowly and deeply into the lungs, where it’s actually needed.

But many people skip the spacer and spray the medication directly into their mouth. That means less medication reaches the airways—and more ends up stuck in the mouth and throat, which can increase irritation and side effects. To use one, attach the inhaler to the spacer, press the inhaler once to release the medication into the chamber, then place your mouth tightly around the mouthpiece and inhale slowly and deeply.

The best strategy for allergy relief

For long-term relief, our experts point to allergen immunotherapy, commonly known as allergy shots. Rather than simply masking symptoms, allergy shots gradually train the immune system to tolerate allergens over time. “When done properly, you essentially teach your body to recognize allergens in a safe, noninflammatory way,” Dr. Shepherd says.

And while it won’t help much this week, allergists agree that allergy medications work best when started before symptoms ramp up. Ideally, treatment should begin about two to four weeks before allergy season starts.

If over-the-counter medications aren’t helping, your symptoms are interfering with daily life or you want more targeted treatment, doctors recommend seeing a board-certified allergist for testing and a personalized treatment plan.

RELATED:

  • Flesh-Eating Bacteria Could Kill Your Summer Plans—Here’s Whether You Should Be Worried About Going to the Beach
  • 11 Ways Your Hotel Room Could Be Making You Sick
  • This Is the Real Reason Candy Doesn’t Taste the Way You Remember It

About the experts

  • An Huynh, MD, is an allergist and clinical immunologist at Dartmouth Hitchcock Medical Center in Lebanon, New Hampshire.
  • Meagan W. Shepherd, MD, is a board-certified allergist and immunologist, a diplomat of the American Board of Allergy and Immunology, and the founder of Shepherd Allergy.
  • Julia L. Montejo, MD is a board-certified allergist and immunologist at Parkview Health in Warsaw, Indiana.

Why trust us

At Reader’s Digest, we’re committed to producing high-quality content by writers with expertise and experience in their field in consultation with relevant, qualified experts. We rely on reputable primary sources, including government and professional organizations and academic institutions as well as our writers’ personal experiences where appropriate. We verify all facts and data, back them with credible sourcing and revisit them over time to ensure they remain accurate and up to date. Read more about our team, our contributors and our editorial policies.

Sources:

  • An Huynh, MD, allergist and clinical immunologist at Dartmouth Hitchcock Medical Center; email interview, May 21, 2026
  • Meagan W. Shepherd, MD, board-certified allergist and immunologist; email interview, May 21, 2026
  • Julia L. Montejo, MD, board-certified allergist and immunologist at Parkview Health; email interview, May 21, 2026
  • Allergy & Asthma Network: “Allergy Statistics in the U.S.”

articles.title: Prince William Answers the Big Question: Will He Be Attending Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s Wedding?
articles.content:
说到名人婚礼，没有比泰勒·斯威夫特和特拉维斯·凯尔西的婚礼更大的了。在排行榜上的知名度、NFL超级明星身份以及他们共同的A级朋友群体，粉丝们已经在想象宾客名单会是什么样子。但有一个可能的回复引起了人们的极大兴趣：威尔士亲王和凯特王妃是否会参加美国最受欢迎的权势夫妇之一的婚礼？

事实证明，这个想法可能并不像听起来那么牵强。再加上夏洛特公主对泰勒·斯威夫特的痴迷，这种潜在的皇室婚礼跨界似乎完美无缺。那么威尔士家族和泰勒·斯威夫特之间有什么联系，我们真的能看到他们一起庆祝这个大日子吗？请继续阅读以了解详情。

据威尔士亲王称，目前还没有确认，但他肯定没有排除这个想法——至少听起来他希望还没有被排除在外！

2026年5月，威廉在英国Heart Breakfast电台节目上出现时，被直接问及是否收到了邀请。“无可奉告，”他微笑着说道，然后补充道，“我希望，我相信可能会有邀请函，但我们会看到的。”

实际上，威尔士家族出现在嘉宾名单上是有充分理由的。威廉此前曾表示，他的孩子们——乔治王子、夏洛特公主和路易斯王子都是泰勒·斯威夫特的超级粉丝，尤其是夏洛特，她“痴迷”于这位歌手。

斯威夫特也暗示过这场婚礼可能会变得多大。在2025年10月《格雷厄姆·诺顿秀》的一次亮相中，她表示自己更喜欢大型婚礼，因为小规模婚礼通常伴随着压力山大的宾客名单决策。她说：“我认为唯一有压力的婚礼是那些你只有少量客人，而人们又在犹豫是否邀请你评估或审视你与他们的关系以确定他们是否应该在场的那些婚礼，”她说。“我不会那样做的。”

因此，虽然关于皇室出席尚未确认，但这确实是一个让人觉得几乎不能错过的名人与皇室的交集故事。我们只能拭目以待，看看皇家的RSVP是否会真正到来。

是的，而且不止一次。威廉王子和泰勒·斯威夫特实际上已经多次相遇。他们最早也是最著名的会面之一发生在2013年，当时斯威夫特在肯辛顿宫慈善晚宴上演出。在一个现在已经成为标志性的时刻，她邀请威廉王子上台与邦·乔维一起演唱《活在祈祷中》。粉丝们至今仍津津乐道于这一最出人意料且有趣的配对。

快进到2024年6月，这种联系变得更加私人化。威尔士亲王与凯特王妃和他们的孩子一起观看了斯威夫特在温布利球场的演出。演出结束后，威尔士一家与斯威夫特和凯尔西在后台见面，并合影留念，这些照片迅速走红。

总而言之，皇室成员与斯威夫特和凯尔西有过一些真正友好的时刻，这使得粉丝们不断想象可能会有皇家回复参加年度婚礼的可能性。我们只能等待，看看是否真的会有皇家的RSVP。
articles.summary:
When it comes to celebrity weddings, it doesn’t get much bigger than Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s. Between the chart-topping fame, NFL superstardom and their combined A-list friend groups, fans are already imagining what the guest list could look like. But there’s one possible RSVP that has people especially intrigued: Will Prince William and Princess Kate attend the wedding of one of America’s favorite power couples?

Turns out, that idea may not be as far-fetched as it sounds. Add in Princess Charlotte’s very relatable Swiftie obsession, and suddenly this potential royal wedding crossover feels oddly perfect. So what’s the connection between the Wales family and Taylor Swift, and could we really see them celebrating the big day together? Read on to find out.

Get Reader’s Digest’s Read Up newsletter for more royals, entertainment, fun facts, humor, cleaning, travel and tech all week long.

Will Prince William and Princess Kate attend Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce’s wedding?

According to the Prince of Wales, nothing is confirmed yet, but he definitely hasn’t ruled it out—or at least it sounds like he’s hoping it hasn’t been ruled out!

During a May 2026 appearance on the U.K.’s Heart Breakfast radio show, William was directly asked whether he had received an invitation. “No comment,” he said with a smile, before adding, “I’m hoping, and I’m sure there might be an invitation around, but we’ll see.”

There’s actually a strong argument for why the Wales family could end up on the guest list. William previously said that his children—Prince George, Princess Charlotte and Prince Louis—are huge Taylor Swift fans, and Charlotte, in particular, is “obsessed” with the singer.

Swift has also hinted at just how big this wedding could become. During an October 2025 appearance on The Graham Norton Show, she suggested she prefers large weddings because smaller ones often come with stressful guest-list decisions. “I think the only stressful weddings are the ones where you have a small amount and people are on the bubble and you have to evaluate or assess your relationship with them to see if they should be there,” she said. “I’m not going to do that.”

So while nothing has been confirmed regarding royal attendance, it’s one of those celebrity-meets-royalty storylines that feels almost too good not to happen. We’ll just have to wait and see if a royal RSVP really comes through.

Have the royals met Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce before?

Yes, and not just once. Prince William and Taylor Swift have actually crossed paths multiple times over the years. One of their earliest and most famous meetups happened in 2013, when Swift performed at a charity gala at Kensington Palace. In a now-iconic moment, she invited William onstage to join Jon Bon Jovi for a rendition of “Livin’ on a Prayer.” Fans still look back on it fondly as one of the most unexpected—and fun!—pairings ever.

Fast-forward to June 2024, when the connection became even more personal. The Prince of Wales attended Swift’s Eras Tour concert at Wembley Stadium with Kate and their children. After the show, the family met Swift and Kelce backstage, posing for photos that quickly went viral.

All in all, the royals have crossed paths with Swift and Kelce in some genuinely friendly moments, making it easy to see why fans keep imagining there could be a royal RSVP to the wedding of the year.

RELATED:

  • Here’s What One Royal *Really* Thought About Her Engagement Ring
  • Royal Scoop! Queen Elizabeth Kept a Secret Stash of This Sweet Treat in the Palace
  • Prince William Banned This Relative from His Wedding—And It’s Not Who You Think!

Why trust us

Reader’s Digest has published hundreds of stories on the British royal family, providing a behind-the-scenes look at the fascinating facets of the monarchy. We regularly cover topics including the latest royal news, the history and meaning behind time-honored traditions, and the everyday quirks of everyone’s favorite family members, from Queen Elizabeth’s daily snack to Prince William’s confessions about his home life. We’re committed to producing high-quality content by writers with expertise and experience in their field in consultation with relevant, qualified experts. We rely on reputable primary sources, including government and professional organizations and academic institutions as well as our writers’ personal experiences where appropriate. We verify all facts and data, back them with credible sourcing and revisit them over time to ensure they remain accurate and up to date. Read more about our team, our contributors and our editorial policies.

articles.title: This Request About Costco’s Free Samples Drives Workers Crazy—Are You Guilty of It?
articles.content:
除了1.5美元的热狗套餐和令人难以拒绝的超值优惠之外，免费试吃也是购买好市多会员卡的最佳理由之一。最近几次购物，我享用了美味的鸡翅、芝士通心粉和饺子——几乎足够当一顿饭了。我总是很感激这些免费小食，也一定会说声谢谢（这是我妈妈从小教导我的）。

但事实证明，并非所有好市多顾客都遵守同样的规则。近日，一名员工在Reddit上发帖吐槽一种让他很反感的请求。继续往下看，了解这个请求是什么——也许你不知不觉中也犯了同样的错误。

订阅Reader's Digest的Read Up通讯，获取更多好市多新闻、清洁技巧、旅行、科技和趣味知识。

要求定制免费试吃品可不太受欢迎。Reddit用户Osh_Kosh_Bigosh在r/Costco板块发了一个帖子，标题是“不，我不会为你定制免费试吃品。”他们列举了被要求定制试吃的各种方式，包括：“能只给我布里奶酪不要饼干吗？”“能让我的芝士墨西哥饼更脆一点吗？”“我想要多加点莎莎酱在薯片上！”

来自俱乐部演示服务公司（CDS）——好市多雇用来准备和分发免费试吃的公司——的其他员工也在同一帖子下分享了各自的经历。用户WantDastardlyBack讲述了一位顾客曾要求他们“立刻准备好所有口味的安妮通心粉”，这样她就可以在决定购买之前把三种口味都尝一遍。

正如Osh_Kosh_Bigosh所解释的，“免费试吃品不存在任何定制或修改。”我对此表示赞同。这是免费的！应该心存感激有人愿意递给你一个免费的饺子或一块巧克力。在ShopRite、阿尔迪或其他普通杂货店可没有这种待遇。这位Reddit用户继续写道：“……试吃品不是'随你所欲'，而是'接受提供的，或者走开什么都别拿。'”

要求定制试吃品不仅仅是令人厌烦；根据Reddit用户Legitimate-Fix-3987的说法，这可能会让CDS员工陷入麻烦。“如果你做了定制试吃品，被CDS经理发现了，他们要么让你回家，要么减少你下周的工时，要么在办公室谈话，要么以上全部。因为遵循DPIS（演示产品信息表）是一条严格的、不可协商的规则。”

除此之外，这对你自己也可能不好。肉类和海鲜尤其需要烹饪到一定温度才能安全食用，所以催促员工可能不是最明智的做法。

虽然要求定制试吃品不会让你直接被好市多禁止入内（据我们所知，唯一可能导致这种后果的事情是滥用该店慷慨的退货政策），但CDS员工可能会拒绝给你试吃品。

如果你有食物过敏或不耐受的情况，你当然有权询问成分。如果你带着孩子购物，可能更容易获得定制试吃品。同一个Reddit帖子上的许多评论者提到，他们的孩子曾被提供不带辣酱的试吃品，或者不带奶酪的饼干。

不言而喻的是，在拿免费试吃品时应该说“请”和“谢谢”。你还应该耐心排队——不要冲到队伍前面——并确保你的购物车整齐地放在一边，不要挡住整个过道。自己扔掉用过的餐具和容器也会让你加分。大多数试吃台旁边都设有垃圾桶，所以不要让CDS员工帮你扔，更不要把它放在旁边的货架上。

在Reader's Digest，我们致力于由具有专业知识和经验的作家提供高质量内容，并咨询相关合格专家。我们依靠可靠的一手来源，包括政府、专业组织和学术机构，以及作家在适当情况下的个人经历。我们验证所有事实和数据，用可靠来源支持，并随着时间推移进行复查以确保其准确性和时效性。了解更多关于我们团队、贡献者和编辑政策的信息。

这篇标题为《这个关于好市多免费试吃的请求让员工抓狂——你犯过吗？》的文章首次发表于Reader's Digest。
articles.summary:
Next to the $1.50 hot dog combo and bargains that are too good to pass up, the free samples are one of the best reasons to spring for a Costco membership. On recent trips, I’ve enjoyed tasty chicken wings, mac and cheese and dumplings—practically enough treats to make a meal. I’m always grateful for the gratis snack and am always sure to say thank you (as my mom ingrained in me since childhood).

But it turns out that not all Costco shoppers live by the same set of rules. Recently, a worker took to Reddit to air their grievances about a particular request that rubs them the wrong way. Read on to find out what the request is—and if you might unknowingly be guilty of it.

Get Reader’s Digest’s Read Up newsletter for more Costco news, cleaning, travel, tech and fun facts all week long.

What request will get you in trouble with the workers giving out free samples?

Trying to modify your freebie will not go over well. Redditor Osh_Kosh_Bigosh started a thread on r/Costco titled, “No I will not be customizing your FREE SAMPLE.” They went on to list all the ways they’ve been asked to customize samples, including: “Can I get just the brie cheese and no cracker?” “Can I get my quesadilla extra crispy?” and “I want extra salsa on my chips!”

Other workers from Club Demonstration Services (CDS), the company Costco hires to prepare and hand out free samples, chimed in on the same thread to share their own experiences. User WantDastardlyBack recounted how a shopper once demanded they prepare all the flavors of Annie’s Mac and Cheese “immediately so that she could try all three before committing to buy them.”

Why is this so irritating?

As Osh_Kosh_Bigosh goes on to explain, “There’s no such thing as modifications or customizations when it comes to free samples.” And I tend to agree. They’re free! Be grateful that someone wants to hand you a free dumpling or piece of chocolate. That doesn’t happen when you’re at a ShopRite, Aldi or other regular grocery store. The Redditor continues, “… samples aren’t ‘have it your way’ they are ‘Take what’s offered or keep walking and get nothing.'”

Requesting a customized sample is more than just annoying; it could get a CDS worker in trouble, according to Redditor Legitimate-Fix-3987. “If you do custom samples and the CDS manager catches you, they either send you home, reduce your hours for the next week, give you a talk in the office or all of them. Because following the DPIS [Demo Product Information Sheet] is a strict non negotiable rule.”

Beyond that, it could be bad for you. Meat and seafood, in particular, need to be cooked to a certain temperature to be safe to eat, so rushing the workers might not be the smartest move.

Could this behavior actually get you banned?

While asking for customized samples won’t get you banned from Costco outright (about the only thing that we know of that will do that is abusing the store’s generous return policy), the CDS worker may refuse to give you a sample.

Are there any acceptable requests when it comes to free samples?

If you have any food allergies or intolerances, you’re certainly within your rights to ask about ingredients. And if you’re shopping with children, you may have better luck getting a customized sample. A number of commenters on the same Reddit thread mentioned that their children have been offered samples without a spicy sauce that goes along with the bite or a cracker without cheese.

So what is proper free-sample etiquette?

It should go without saying that you should say “please” and “thank you” when taking a free sample. You should also wait your turn patiently—no busting to the front of the line—and make sure your cart is stashed neatly to the side and not blocking a full aisle. Tossing your own utensil and serving container will also score you major brownie points. Most sample stations are set up with a trash can conveniently next to them, so don’t ask the CDS worker to toss it for you or, worse, stash it on an adjacent shelf.

  • The Surprising Reason Why Costco Baked Goods Are Never Piping Hot

Why trust us

At Reader’s Digest, we’re committed to producing high-quality content by writers with expertise and experience in their field in consultation with relevant, qualified experts. We rely on reputable primary sources, including government and professional organizations and academic institutions as well as our writers’ personal experiences where appropriate. We verify all facts and data, back them with credible sourcing and revisit them over time to ensure they remain accurate and up to date. Read more about our team, our contributors and our editorial policies.
